3.3: Species parameterisation for atlantic_salmon

FAO Climate Change Risks to Fed Mariculture

Author

Tormey Reimer

Published

29 June 2026

← Previous: Parameterise species: european_seabass

Next: Parameterise species: rainbow_trout →

Overview

This document runs the checking and calibration process on the atlantic_salmon model. For a full description of the process, see parameterise_species_seabream.

Species parameters

As we are only running a single representative individual, the Monte-Carlo parameters are not in use.

Load species parameters for atlantic_salmon 
this_species <- "atlantic_salmon"

parameters_species <- read_excel(
  file.path(rawdata_path, 'parameters_species.xlsx'),
  sheet = this_species,
  trim_ws = T,
  n_max = 28
) %>%
  clean_names() %>% 
  select(used_in_script, value, flexibility)

species_params <- parameters_species %>% pull(value) %>% as.numeric()
names(species_params) <- parameters_species %>% pull(used_in_script)

species_params[["overFmean"]] <- 0.025
species_params[["overFdelta"]] <- 0.0025

Environmental/feed parameters

Load feed and ingredient data and calculate feed composition parameters 
feeds_df <- read_excel(here("data", "raw_data", "feed_profiles_and_composition.xlsx"), sheet = "all_feeds") %>%
  select(feed, ingredient, proportion) %>% 
  filter(feed == "Atlantic salmon 1")

ingredients_df <- read_excel(here("data", "raw_data", "all_ingredients.xlsx"), sheet = "all_ingredients") %>% 
  select(ingredient, protein, lipid, carb)

digestibility_df <- read_excel(here("data", "raw_data", "all_ingredients.xlsx"), sheet = "all_ingredients") %>% 
  clean_names() %>% 
  rename(
    protein_digestibility = mean_protein_digestibility, 
    lipid_digestibility = mean_lipid_digestibility, 
    carb_digestibility = mean_carb_digestibility
  ) %>% 
  select(ingredient, protein_digestibility, lipid_digestibility, carb_digestibility)

feed_params <- sum_feeds(
  format_feeds(feeds_df, ingredients_df, digestibility_df)
  )[[1]]

qs_save(feed_params, file.path(prepdata_path, "feed_parameters", "feed_parameters_atlantic_salmon.qs"))

In the feed used for this species:

  • Protein is 86.9% digestible
  • Lipid is 95.1% digestible
  • Carbohydrate is 75.2% digestible
Plot feed nutritional profile showing digestible and indigestible fractions 
feed_params %>% 
  map2(., names(feed_params), function(fp, nm) {
    fp %>% mutate(name = nm)
  }) %>% 
  bind_rows() %>% 
  mutate(digestible = macro * digestibility, indigestible = macro - digestible) %>% 
  select(name, indigestible, digestible) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = -name, names_to = "type", values_to = "value") %>% 
  mutate(
    type = factor(type, levels = c("indigestible", "digestible"), labels = c("Indigestible", "Digestible")),
    name = factor(name, levels = c("protein", "lipid", "carb"), labels = c("Protein", "Lipid", "Carbohydrate"))
  ) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = name, y = 100*value, fill = type)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  prettyplot() +
  theme(legend.position = "right", axis.title.x = element_blank()) +
  labs(main = "Feed nutritional profile", y = "Proportion of total feed (%)")
Ignoring unknown labels:
• main : "Feed nutritional profile"

The temperature timeseries used is based on the mean current temperatures at farms assigned to this model group in 02_assigning_locations.

Define simulation times and temperature timeseries 
# Get current mean temperatures for farms assigned to this model
farm_temps_all <- file.path(prepdata_path, "farm_temps_bymodel", "meanyear_farm_temps_atlantic_salmon.qs") %>% 
  qs_read() %>% 
  group_by(model_name, doy) %>% 
  reframe(sst = mean(sst))

farm_temps_all <- rbind(
  farm_temps_all,
  farm_temps_all %>% mutate(doy = doy + 365),
  farm_temps_all %>% mutate(doy = doy + 730)
)

# Get feeding response at each DOY
farm_temps_all$fr <- sapply(
    X = farm_temps_all$sst, 
    FUN = feeding_rate, 
    species_params = species_params
  )
farm_temps_all$fr[is.na(farm_temps_all$fr)] <- 0

# Get a good growing window
grow_days <- species_params[["gd"]] %>% as.integer()
gds <- farm_temps_all %>% 
  mutate(
    roll_fr = slide_dbl(fr, sum, .before = 0, .after = grow_days - 1)
  ) %>%
  slice(1:(nrow(farm_temps_all) - grow_days + 1)) %>%
  slice_max(roll_fr, n = 1) %>%
  mutate(end_doy = doy + grow_days - 1)

times <- c(
  t_start = gds$doy[1],
  t_end   = gds$end_doy[1],
  dt      = 1L
)

farm_temps <- farm_temps_all %>% 
  filter(doy >= times['t_start'] & doy <= times['t_end'])

# ggplot(farm_temps, aes(x = doy, y = sst)) +
#   geom_line() +
#   theme_classic()

Run model with raw parameters

Check that all parameters being passed to the model functions are correct 
check_fish_growth_inputs(
  species_params = species_params,
  water_temp     = farm_temps$sst,
  feed_params    = feed_params,
  times          = c(
    "t_start" = min(farm_temps$doy),
    "t_end" = max(farm_temps$doy),
    "dt" = 1
  ),
  init_weight    = species_params['meanW'],
  ingmax         = species_params['meanImax']
)
All inputs look good — `fish_growth()` should run without errors.
Run fish_growth() for a representative individual 
set.seed(42)
one_fish <- fish_growth(
  species_params = species_params,
  water_temp     = farm_temps$sst,
  feed_params    = feed_params,
  times          = c(
    "t_start" = min(farm_temps$doy),
    "t_end" = max(farm_temps$doy),
    "dt" = 1
  ),
  init_weight    = species_params['meanW'],
  ingmax         = species_params['meanImax']
)

cat(sprintf(
  'Initial weight: %.1f g  |  Final weight: %.1f g (%.1f g)  |  Approx. FCR: %.2f |  Final SGR: %.1f \n',
  one_fish[1, 'weight'],
  tail(one_fish[, 'weight'], 1), species_params['CS'],
  sum(one_fish[, 'food_prov'], na.rm = T) / (tail(one_fish[, 'weight'], 1) - one_fish[1, 'weight']),
  tail(one_fish[, 'SGR'], 1)
))
Initial weight: 125.0 g  |  Final weight: 3424.2 g (4000.0 g)  |  Approx. FCR: 0.96 |  Final SGR: 0.5

The model outputs here are looking very reasonable but it looks like harvest weight is being slightly underestimated (Figure 2).

Model sensitivity

Set uncertainty of parameters (and remove parameters not being tested for sensitivity) 
# "flexibility" refers to the flexibility I'm willing to give each parameter (ie uncertainty)
flex_params <- parameters_species %>% 
  select(param = used_in_script, flexibility) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(flexibility)) %>% 
  arrange(flexibility)
Calculate parameter sensitivity for weight and SGR across plus-minus 10 percent parameter perturbations 
species_params_sens <- map(
  flex_params$param,
  function(nm) {
    map(c(0.9, 1.0, 1.1), function(level) {
      species_params[nm] <- species_params[nm] * level
      species_params
    }) %>% 
      setNames(c("low", "med", "high"))
  }
) %>% 
  setNames(flex_params$param)

set.seed(42)
sens_results <- map2_dfr(
  species_params_sens, 
  names(species_params_sens),
  function(sp, nm) {
    map_dfr(sp, function(sp_lev) {
      fish_growth(
        species_params = sp_lev,
        water_temp     = farm_temps$sst,
        feed_params    = feed_params,
        times          = c(
          "t_start" = min(farm_temps$doy),
          "t_end" = max(farm_temps$doy),
          "dt" = 1
        ),
        init_weight    = sp_lev['meanW'],
        ingmax         = sp_lev['meanImax']
      ) %>% 
        as.data.frame() %>% 
        tail(1) %>% 
        select(weight, SGR)
    }) %>% 
      mutate(
        param = nm, 
        level = names(sp)
      )
  }
) 

# Calculate sensitivity
sens_results <- sens_results %>%
  group_by(param) %>%
  summarise(across(c(weight, SGR), ~ (.x[level == "high"] - .x[level == "low"]) / (.x[level == "med"] * 0.2), .names = "{.col}")) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = !contains("param"),
    values_to = "sens", names_to = "output",
    names_transform = c(output = as.factor)
  ) %>% 
  left_join(flex_params, by = "param") %>% 
  mutate(param = factor(param, levels = rev(flex_params$param)))

Figure 3 shows how sensitive the model output (individual weight/SGR) is to changes in each parameter - ie the positive/negative impact of increasing that parameter by 10%.

Plot parameter sensitivity as a barplot facetted by output variable 
sens_results %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = param, y = sens, fill = output)) +
  geom_col(colour = "black") +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~output) +
  prettyplot()

Hypercube adjustment

The hypercube adjustment uses Latin Hypercube Sampling (LHS) to search for parameter combinations that bring model output closer to the target weight (commercial size).

Calculate per-parameter sampling ranges from sensitivity and flexibility 
# Combine sensitivity across both output variables (mean absolute sensitivity)
sens_combined <- sens_results %>%
  group_by(param, flexibility) %>%
  summarise(mean_abs_sens = mean(abs(sens)), .groups = "drop")

# Range score: inversely proportional to mean absolute sensitivity and flexibility rank.
# Cap 1/sens to avoid blow-up when sensitivity is near zero.
max_inv_sens <- 20
min_inv_sens <- 0.1
sens_combined <- sens_combined %>%
  mutate(
    inv_sens  = pmax(min_inv_sens, pmin(1 / pmax(mean_abs_sens, 1 / max_inv_sens), max_inv_sens)),
    raw_range = inv_sens * (1 / flexibility)
  )

# Cap range changes at +-10%
sens_combined <- sens_combined %>%
  mutate(
    param        = as.character(param),
    sample_range = scales::rescale(raw_range, to = c(0.005, 0.10))
  )
Plot sampling ranges per parameter 
sens_combined %>%
  mutate(param = factor(param, levels = param[order(sample_range)])) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = param, y = sample_range * 100)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2171b5", colour = "black", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Parameter", y = "Sampling range (+-% of baseline)") +
  theme_classic()
Define target experimental data as a list of experiments - each with its own temperature timeseries and named target outputs 
# Each entry in `target_experiments` represents one experimental condition.
# `water_temp` : data.frame with columns `day` (integer) and `temp_C` (numeric)
#                matching that experiment's temperature conditions.
# `targets`    : a named numeric vector of any model output columns to fit.
#                Different experiments can supply different (or overlapping) outputs.

# SS fitness is computed as the MEAN squared error across all (experiment × output)
# observations, so experiments with more targets do not dominate the fit.
target_experiments <- list(
  list(
    water_temp = farm_temps$sst,
    targets    = c(weight_change = unname(species_params["CS"] - species_params["meanW"])),
    meanW      = species_params["meanW"]
  )
)

n_pairs <- length(target_experiments)

# This function processes the fish_growth() output to get it ready for comparison. It's defined here so that if different comparisons are added in this chunk I can change the calculation everywhere at once
get_comparison_stats <- function(df) {
  dfdf <- as.data.frame(df)
  data.frame(
    weight_change = tail(dfdf$weight, 1) - head(dfdf$weight, 1),
    SGR = tail(dfdf$SGR, 1)
  )
}
Generate LHS parameter samples and run model for each (1000 samples x n_pairs with progress updates every 100) 
n_samples     <- 2500
param_names   <- sens_combined$param
sample_ranges <- sens_combined$sample_range
n_params      <- length(param_names)

set.seed(42)
lhs_raw <- randomLHS(n_samples, n_params)

# Map [0, 1] to [1 - range, 1 + range] multipliers for each parameter
multiplier_matrix <- sweep(lhs_raw * 2 - 1, 2, sample_ranges, "*") + 1
colnames(multiplier_matrix) <- param_names

# Run model once per experiment for each LHS sample.
# All final-row model output columns are retained so any named target can be used.
lhs_results <- vector("list", n_samples)
start_time  <- proc.time()["elapsed"]

for (i in seq_len(n_samples)) {
  if (i %% 500 == 0) {
    elapsed <- round(proc.time()["elapsed"] - start_time, 1)
    rate    <- i / elapsed
    eta     <- round((n_samples - i) / rate, 1)
    cat(sprintf(
      "Sample %4d / %d | elapsed: %.0fs | ETA: %.0fs\n",
      i, n_samples, elapsed, eta
    ))
  }

  sp <- species_params
  for (j in param_names) {
    sp[j] <- species_params[j] * multiplier_matrix[i, j]
  }

  # Run once per experiment; keep all output columns from the final timestep
  pair_results <- vector("list", n_pairs)
  for (k in seq_len(n_pairs)) {
    wt <- target_experiments[[k]]$water_temp

    change_params <- names(target_experiments[[k]])[names(target_experiments[[k]]) %in% names(species_params)]
    if (length(change_params) > 0) {
      for (c in change_params) {
        sp[c] <- unlist(target_experiments[[k]][c])
      }      
    }

    result <- tryCatch(
      fish_growth(
        species_params = sp,
        water_temp     = wt,
        feed_params    = feed_params,
        times          = c(
          "t_start" = 1,
          "t_end"   = length(wt),
          "dt"      = 1
        ),
        init_weight = sp["meanW"],
        ingmax      = sp["meanImax"]
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (!is.null(result)) {
      final <- get_comparison_stats(result)
      pair_results[[k]] <- final %>% mutate(sample = i, pair = k, .before = 1)
    }
  }

  pair_df <- bind_rows(pair_results)
  # Only retain samples where all experiments completed successfully
  if (nrow(pair_df) == n_pairs) {
    lhs_results[[i]] <- pair_df
  }
}
Sample  500 / 2500 | elapsed: 18s | ETA: 70s
Sample 1000 / 2500 | elapsed: 34s | ETA: 51s
Sample 1500 / 2500 | elapsed: 50s | ETA: 34s
Sample 2000 / 2500 | elapsed: 68s | ETA: 17s
Sample 2500 / 2500 | elapsed: 85s | ETA: 0s
Generate LHS parameter samples and run model for each (1000 samples x n_pairs with progress updates every 100) 
cat(sprintf(
  "Done. Total time: %.1fs\n",
  proc.time()["elapsed"] - start_time
))
Done. Total time: 84.6s
Generate LHS parameter samples and run model for each (1000 samples x n_pairs with progress updates every 100) 
# One row per (sample, experiment)
lhs_results_df <- bind_rows(lhs_results)
Calculate absolute weight difference between modelled and target weight change for each LHS sample 
target_wt_change <- target_experiments[[1]]$targets[["weight_change"]]

# Absolute difference between modelled and target weight change per sample
lhs_fit_df <- lhs_results_df %>%
  group_by(sample) %>%
  summarise(abs_diff = abs(weight_change - target_wt_change), .groups = "drop")

# Attach multipliers for inspection of top-performing samples
multipliers_df <- as.data.frame(multiplier_matrix) %>%
  mutate(sample = seq_len(n_samples))

lhs_fit_df <- lhs_fit_df %>%
  left_join(multipliers_df, by = "sample")

# Baseline: run the unadjusted model and compute absolute difference
baseline_result <- fish_growth(
  species_params = species_params,
  water_temp     = target_experiments[[1]]$water_temp,
  feed_params    = feed_params,
  times          = c(
    "t_start" = 1,
    "t_end"   = length(target_experiments[[1]]$water_temp),
    "dt"      = 1
  ),
  init_weight = species_params["meanW"],
  ingmax      = species_params["meanImax"]
)

baseline_abs_diff <- abs(get_comparison_stats(baseline_result)$weight_change - target_wt_change)

cat(sprintf(
  "Samples completed: %d / %d\n",
  n_distinct(lhs_fit_df$sample), n_samples
))
Samples completed: 2500 / 2500

Figure 5 shows the distribution of absolute weight differences across all samples.

Plot distribution of absolute weight differences across all LHS samples 
lhs_fit_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = abs_diff)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "#2171b5", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = baseline_abs_diff, colour = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "Absolute weight difference (g)", y = "Count") +
  theme_classic()

The parameter multipliers for the best-fitting sample (relative to baseline) are:

Show parameter multipliers for the best-fitting sample 
best_sample <- lhs_fit_df %>%
  arrange(abs_diff) %>%
  slice_head(n = 1) %>%
  pull(sample)

best_lhs_results <- lhs_fit_df %>%
  filter(sample == best_sample) %>%
  select(all_of(param_names)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "param", values_to = "multiplier") 
  
# If the change is extremely small (<0.05% of baseline) then the baseline version is kept
best_lhs_results <- best_lhs_results %>%
  mutate(
    multiplier    = case_when(round(multiplier, 2) == 1.00 ~ 1, T ~ multiplier),
    pct_change    = (multiplier - 1) * 100,
    baseline_val  = species_params[param],
    adjusted_val  = baseline_val * multiplier
  ) %>%
  filter(multiplier != 1) %>% 
  arrange(desc(abs(pct_change)))

best_lhs_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(multiplier, pct_change), decimals = 2) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(baseline_val, adjusted_val), decimals = 4) %>% 
  cols_label(
    param        = "Parameter",
    multiplier   = "Multiplier",
    pct_change   = "% change",
    baseline_val = "Raw value",
    adjusted_val = "Calibrated value"
  )

Calibrated run

Replace calibrated species parameters with new values 
new_params <- best_lhs_results$adjusted_val
names(new_params) <- best_lhs_results$param
species_params_new <- species_params
for(nm in names(new_params)) {
  species_params_new[nm] <- new_params[nm]
}
species_params_new["gd"] <- round(species_params_new["gd"], 0)

spn <- data.frame(
  Parameter = names(species_params_new),
  Original_Value = unname(species_params),
  New_Value = unname(species_params_new),
  Pct_Change = 100*unname((species_params-species_params_new)/species_params)
)
Run fish_growth() with the calibrated parameter values 
# Run with the default temperature timeseries for the trajectory plot (same as
# the initial checks above), giving a single representative growth trajectory.
set.seed(42)

times <- c(
  "t_start" = gds$doy[1],
  "t_end"   = gds$doy[1] + unname(species_params_new["gd"]) - 1,
  "dt"      = 1
)

farm_temps_new <- farm_temps_all %>% 
  filter(doy >= times['t_start'] & doy <= times['t_end'])

one_fish_new <- fish_growth(
  species_params = species_params_new,
  water_temp     = farm_temps_new$sst,
  feed_params    = feed_params,
  times          = times,
  init_weight    = species_params_new['meanW'],
  ingmax         = species_params_new['meanImax']
)

# Also validate against each paired experimental temperature series to confirm
# that the best parameter set improves fit across all observed conditions.
calibrated_pair_results <- map_dfr(seq_len(n_pairs), function(k) {
  wt <- target_experiments[[k]]$water_temp
  sp <- species_params_new
  if (!is.null(target_experiments[[k]]$initW)) {sp["meanW"] <- target_experiments[[k]]$initW}
  set.seed(42)
  result <- fish_growth(
    species_params = sp,
    water_temp     = wt,
    feed_params    = feed_params,
    times          = c(
      "t_start" = 1,
      "t_end"   = length(wt),
      "dt"      = 1
    ),
    init_weight = sp['meanW'],
    ingmax      = sp['meanImax']
  )
  get_comparison_stats(result) %>% mutate(pair = k)
})

# Summary comparison: calibrated vs. target per experiment
# Only columns that appear in each experiment's `targets` vector are shown.
calibrated_summary <- map_dfr(seq_len(n_pairs), function(k) {
  row  <- calibrated_pair_results %>% filter(pair == k)
  tgts <- target_experiments[[k]]$targets
  map_dfr(names(tgts), function(col) {
    data.frame(
      pair      = k,
      output    = col,
      modelled  = row[[col]],
      target    = tgts[[col]],
      diff      = row[[col]] - tgts[[col]]
    )
  })
})
Display modelled vs target values for each experiment after calibration 
calibrated_summary %>%
  gt() %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(modelled, target, diff), decimals = 2) %>% 
  cols_label(
    pair     = "Run",
    output   = "Output variable",
    modelled = "Modelled",
    target   = "Target",
    diff     = "Difference"
  )

Figure 6 shows all output variables over the grow-out period.

Save calibrated species parameters 
qd_save(
  species_params_new, 
  file.path(prepdata_path, "species_parameters", "species_parameters_atlantic_salmon.qs")
)
